context.pr_title: feat: Switch to enum queue mode, add 'replace' mode.
context.pr_body:
Follow up to https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/pull/1879

Kind of janky because of the fact that job queue tasks are split up before the client- I make clear-queue a property of the first job.

Not sure if the enum is in the right place.

Tentatively planning to implement ctrl-click as "Alternate queue mode".

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README

Generative AI for Krita

✨[Features](#features) | ⭳ [Download](https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/releases/latest) | 🛠️[Installation](https://docs.interstice.cloud/installation) | 🎞️ [Video](https://youtu.be/Ly6USRwTHe0) | 🖼️[Gallery](#gallery) | 📖[User Guide](https://docs.interstice.cloud) | 💬[Discussion](https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/discussions) | 🗣️[Discord](https://discord.gg/pWyzHfHHhU) This is a plugin to use generative AI in image painting and editing workflows from within Krita. Visit [**www.interstice.cloud**](https://www.interstice.cloud) for an introduction. Learn how to install and use it on [**docs.interstice.cloud**](https://docs.interstice.cloud). The main goals of this project are: * **Precision and Control.** Creating entire images from text can be unpredictable. To get the result you envision, you can restrict generation to selections, refine existing content with a variable degree of strength, focus text on image regions, and guide generation with reference images, sketches, line art, depth maps, and more. * **Workflow Integration.** Most image generation tools focus heavily on AI parameters. This project aims to be an unobtrusive tool that integrates and synergizes with image editing workflows in Krita. Draw, paint, edit and generate seamlessly without worrying about resolution and technical details. * **Local, Open, Free.** We are committed to open source models. Customize presets, bring your own models, and run everything local on your hardware. Cloud generation is also available to get started quickly without heavy investment. [![Watch video demo](media/screenshot-video-preview.webp)](https://youtu.be/Ly6USRwTHe0 "Watch video demo") ## Features * **Inpainting**: Use selections for generative fill, expand, to add or remove objects * **Live Painting**: Let AI interpret your canvas in real time for immediate feedback. [Watch Video](https://youtu.be/AF2VyqSApjA?si=Ve5uQJWcNOATtABU) * **Upscaling**: Upscale and enrich images to 4k, 8k and beyond without running out of memory. * **Diffusion Models**: Supports Stable Diffusion 1.5, and XL. Partial support for [Flux](https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/discussions/1176) and SD3. * **ControlNet**: Scribble, Line art, Canny edge, Pose, Depth, Normals, Segmentation, +more * **IP-Adapter**: Reference images, Style and composition transfer, Face swap * **Regions**: Assign individual text descriptions to image areas defined by layers. * **Job Queue**: Queue and cancel generation jobs while working on your image. * **History**: Preview results and browse previous generations and prompts at any time. * **Strong Defaults**: Versatile default style presets allow for a streamlined UI. * **Customization**: Create your own presets - custom checkpoints, LoRA, samplers and more. ## Getting Started See the [Plugin Install ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods

arguments.reason: Enum may belong in shared properties; need to see how properties/enums are defined.
arguments.expected_information: Patterns for enums, serialization, UI binding.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`ai_diffusion/properties.py` defines a small property/binding framework for PyQt models: observable properties with change signals, UI binding helpers, and persistence-oriented serialization/deserialization (including `Enum` handling).

### Architecture
- **Observable property system**: Classes inherit `ObservableProperties` and declare attributes as `Property(...)`. At subclass creation time (`ObservableProperties.__init_subclass__`), each `Property` descriptor is replaced with a `PropertyImpl` (a real Python `property`) and a corresponding backing field `_{name}` is created on the class with the default value.
- **Change notification**: `PropertyImpl._setter` writes the backing field and emits `{name}_changed` on the instance. If the property is marked `persist=True`, it additionally emits a generic `modified` signal (if present) with `(instance, property_name)`.
- **Qt UI binding**: `bind`, `bind_combo`, and `bind_toggle` connect model signals to widget setters (and optionally back from widget to model) using a `Bind` mode and return either a raw `QMetaObject.Connection` or a `Binding` object that can disconnect both directions.
- **Persistence**: `serialize` and `deserialize` operate only on properties marked persistent via `PropertyImpl.persist`, with special casing for `Enum` and `QUuid`, plus optional custom converter hooks.

### Functions & Classes
- `ObservableProperties` — Mixin that auto-installs observable properties into subclasses.
  - `ObservableProperties.__init_subclass__` — Core metaprogramming hook: finds `Property` declarations, creates `_{name}` defaults (via `_copy_reference_types`), resolves optional custom getter/setter method names, and replaces each `Property` with `PropertyImpl`. This is the key pattern for how properties are defined in the project.

- `Property` — Declarative property descriptor used in class bodies.
  - `Property.__init__` — Stores `default_value`, optional `getter`/`setter` (method names), and `persist` flag. It’s a placeholder descriptor that is intended to be replaced by `PropertyImpl`.

- `PropertyImpl` — Runtime implementation of an observable property (subclasses `property`).
  - `PropertyImpl.__init__` — Builds the `property` with either custom getter/setter or default `_getter`/`_setter`, and stores `name` and `persist`.
  - `PropertyImpl._getter` — Reads the backing field `_{name}` from the instance.
  - `PropertyImpl._setter` — Writes the backing field if the value changed, emits `{name}_changed(value)`, and if `persist` is true, emits `modified(instance, name)` when that signal exists. This is central to UI updates and persistence dirty-tracking.

- `Binding` (NamedTuple) — Container for two Qt connections (model→widget and widget→model).
  - `Binding.disconnect` — Disconnects both stored connections.
  - `Binding.disconnect_all` — Utility to disconnect a sequence of either raw `QMetaObject.Connection` or `Binding` instances.

- `Bind` (Enum) — Binding direction modes.
  - `Bind.one_way` — Only model→widget.
  - `Bind.two_way` — Model→widget and widget→model.

- `bind(model, model_property: str, widget, widget_property: str, mode=Bind.two_way)` — Generic property binding.
  - Connects model’s change signal (via `_signal`) to widget setter (via `_setter`), pushes initial model value to widget, and optionally wires widget changes back to model. Returns a single connection for one-way, or a `Binding` for two-way.

- `bind_combo(model, model_property: str, combo: QComboBox, mode=Bind.two_way)` — Specialized binding for `QComboBox`.
  - Uses `combo.findData(value)` / `combo.currentData()` mapping rather than index/text. Two-way uses `combo.currentIndexChanged`.

- `bind_toggle(model, model_property: str, widget, mode=Bind.two_way)` — Specialized binding for boolean toggle widgets.
  - Uses widget’s `checked` property / `setChecked` and `widget.toggled` signal.

- `_signal(inst, property: str) -> pyqtBoundSignal` — Signal naming adapter.
  - Prefers `{property}_changed`, otherwise falls back to Qt-style `{property}Changed`. Important for consistent binding across custom models vs Qt objects.

- `_setter(inst, property: str)` — Setter adapter.
  - Uses Qt-style `set{Property}` if present, otherwise falls back to `setattr(inst, property, value)`.

- `is_persistent(obj: QObject, name: str)` — Detects persistence eligibility.
  - Checks the class dict for `name` and confirms it is a `PropertyImpl` with `persist=True`.

- `_default_serializer(value)` / `_default_deserializer(type, value)` — Identity hooks.
  - Default converter functions for `serialize`/`deserialize` customization.

- `serialize(obj: QObject, converter=_default_serializer)` — Serializes persistent properties to a dict.
  - For each persistent property: if value is an `Enum`, stores `value.value`; if `QUuid`, stores `toString()`; else applies `converter`.

- `deserialize(obj: QObject, data: dict[str, Any], converter=_default_deserializer)` — Loads persistent properties from a dict.
  - For each key in `data` that is persistent: detects current attribute type, reconstructs `Enum` via `current.__class__(value)` and `QUuid` via `QUuid(value)`, applies `converter(type(current), value)`, enforces type match, then `setattr`.

- `_copy_reference_types(object)` — Defensive copying for defaults.
  - Copies `list`/`dict` defaults so class-level defaults don’t get shared mutably.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- **Enums and persistence**: This file already implements a clear pattern for `Enum` persistence: `serialize` writes `Enum.value`, and `deserialize` reconstructs with `EnumClass(value)`. If an enum “belongs in shared properties,” this is the main place it will interact with persistence and UI binding expectations.
- **Patterns for enums/serialization/UI binding**: `Bind` shows how enums are currently used as configuration; `serialize`/`deserialize` show the expected conversion semantics for enums; and the `bind*` helpers show how model properties are expected to expose change signals (`{name}_changed` / `{name}Changed`) for UI wiring.
- **Where to integrate new/shared enums**: If introducing additional shared enums or changing enum serialization behavior, changes would likely need to remain compatible with the `Enum.value` round-trip used here, and with `deserialize`’s strict type checking (`isinstance(value, type(current))`).

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`ai_diffusion/jobs.py` defines the in-document job model and a Qt-signal-driven `JobQueue` that tracks queued/executing/finished jobs, their results, selection state, and history pruning based on memory limits.

### Architecture
- **Data layer:** `JobRegion` and `JobParams` are `@dataclass` containers used to serialize/deserialize job configuration (including backward-compat migration via `from_dict` and `_move_field`).
- **Job model:** `Job` holds identity, `JobKind`, `JobState`, parameters, timestamps, produced `ImageCollection` results, and per-result usage flags.
- **Queue manager (Qt):** `JobQueue(QObject)` maintains an internal `deque[Job]`, emits Qt signals for UI updates and lifecycle events, manages selection of result items, and prunes stored history based on `settings.history_size`.

### Functions & Classes
- `JobState(Flag)` — Represents lifecycle state: `queued`, `executing`, `finished`, `cancelled`. Used throughout `JobQueue` to count jobs and to drive cancellation logic.
- `JobKind(Enum)` — Classifies jobs: `diffusion`, `control_layer`, `upscaling`, `live_preview`, `animation_batch`, `animation_frame`, `animation`. Controls special handling in `JobQueue` (history retention, memory accounting, immediate removal for non-diffusion/animation).
- `JobRegion` — Region-specific generation settings (`layer_id`, `prompt`, `bounds`, `is_background`). Important for “task splitting” scenarios where a single job may target multiple regions.
  - `JobRegion.from_dict` — Rehydrates `bounds` into a `Bounds` instance and constructs a `JobRegion`.
- `JobParams` — Main parameter bundle for a job: `bounds`, `name`, `regions`, `metadata`, `seed`, `has_mask`, `frame`, `animation_id`. This is the primary object that would carry any queue-mode or splitting-related flags if added.
  - `JobParams.from_dict` — Deserializes `bounds`/`regions` and migrates older documents (pre-1.26.0) by moving legacy top-level fields (`prompt`, `negative_prompt`, `strength`, `style`, `sampler`, `checkpoint`) into `metadata` using `_move_field`.
  - `JobParams.equal_ignore_seed` — Compares two `JobParams` ignoring `seed` (useful for detecting “same job” changes without treating reseeding as a change).
  - `JobParams.set_style` — Writes style/checkpoint/loras/sampler description into `metadata`.
  - `JobParams.prompt` (property) — Convenience accessor for `metadata["prompt"]`.
  - `JobParams.style` (property) — Convenience accessor for `metadata["style"]`.
  - `JobParams.strength` (property) — Convenience accessor for `metadata["strength"]` with default `1.0`.
- `Job` — Runtime representation of a queued/executing/finished job, including produced images and result usage tracking.
  - `Job.__init__` — Initializes job identity, kind, params, timestamp, empty `ImageCollection`, and `in_use` map.
  - `Job.result_was_used` — Checks whether a particular result index was marked used.
- `JobQueue(QObject)` — Central queue/history manager per document, exposing Qt signals for UI and other components.
  - `JobQueue.Item(NamedTuple)` — Identifies a specific result image by `(job, image)` (job id string + image index).
  - Signals:  
    - `count_changed` — queue length/state changed  
    - `selection_changed` — selection changed  
    - `job_finished` / `job_discarded` — lifecycle events for jobs  
    - `result_used` / `result_discarded` — per-result events
  - `JobQueue.__init__` — Creates `_entries` deque, selection tracking, and `_memory_usage` counter (MB).
  - `JobQueue.add` — Convenience wrapper creating a `Job(None, kind, params)` and enqueuing it.
  - `JobQueue.add_control` — Creates a `control_layer` job with a formatted name and attaches `job.control`.
  - `JobQueue.add_job` — Appends a `Job` to `_entries` and emits `count_changed`.
  - `JobQueue.remove` — Removes a job from `_entries` and emits `count_changed` (note comment: diffusion/animation are “kept for history” elsewhere; others removed on finish).
  - `JobQueue.find` — Looks up a job by `id`.
  - `JobQueue.count` — Counts jobs with an exact `state` identity match (`is state`).
  - `JobQueue.has_item` — Validates that a `JobQueue.Item` points to an existing job and result index.
  - `JobQueue.set_results` — Stores results on the job; for `JobKind.diffusion` and `JobKind.animation` it increments `_memory_usage` and calls `prune(keep=job)`.
  - `JobQueue.notify_started` — Sets state to `executing` (if not already) and emits `count_changed`.
  - `JobQueue.notify_finished` — Marks `finished`, emits `job_finished`, calls `_cancel_earlier_jobs`, emits `count_changed`, and immediately `remove`s jobs not in `[JobKind.diffusion, JobKind.animation]`.
  - `JobQueue.notify_cancelled` — Marks `cancelled`, calls `_cancel_earlier_jobs`, emits `count_changed`.
  - `JobQueue.notify_used` — Marks a specific result index as used and emits `result_used`.
  - `JobQueue.select` — Sets selection to a single `(job_id, index)` item.
  - `JobQueue.toggle_selection` — Toggles selection off/on, remembering the previous selection if still valid.
  - `JobQueue._discard_job` — Internal removal that also decrements `_memory_usage` by job results size and emits `job_discarded`.
  - `JobQueue.prune` — Discards oldest jobs while `_memory_usage > settings.history_size`, but never discards the `keep` job; uses `_entries[0]` as the oldest.

  - `JobQueue.__len__`, `JobQueue.__getitem__`, `JobQueue.__iter__` — Container-like access over `_entries`.
  - `JobQueue.selection` (property + setter) — Stores selection list and emits `selection_changed` on change.
  - `JobQueue.memory_usage` (property) — Exposes `_memory_usage` (MB).
  - `JobQueue._cancel_earlier_jobs` — Cancels any earlier jobs still `queued` or `executing` before a given job, to recover from server disconnect situations.
- `_move_field` — Helper for `JobParams.from_dict` migration: moves a key from `src` to `dest` if present.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal

The closest concepts are `JobKind` (affects retention/removal) and `JobState` (lifecycle), plus the `_cancel_earlier_jobs` recovery behavior that implicitly changes queue semantics after disconnects.

### File Overview
`ai_diffusion/jobs.py` defines the in-document job model and a Qt-signal-driven `JobQueue` that tracks queued/executing/finished jobs, their results, selection state, and history pruning based on memory limits.

### Architecture
- **Data layer:** `JobRegion` and `JobParams` are `@dataclass` containers used to serialize/deserialize job configuration (including backward-compat migration via `from_dict` and `_move_field`).
- **Job model:** `Job` holds identity, `JobKind`, `JobState`, parameters, timestamps, produced `ImageCollection` results, and per-result usage flags.
- **Queue manager (Qt):** `JobQueue(QObject)` maintains an internal `deque[Job]`, emits Qt signals for UI updates and lifecycle events, manages selection of result items, and prunes stored history based on `settings.history_size`.

### Functions & Classes
- `JobState(Flag)` — Represents lifecycle state: `queued`, `executing`, `finished`, `cancelled`. Used throughout `JobQueue` to count jobs and to drive cancellation logic.
- `JobKind(Enum)` — Classifies jobs: `diffusion`, `control_layer`, `upscaling`, `live_preview`, `animation_batch`, `animation_frame`, `animation`. Controls special handling in `JobQueue` (history retention, memory accounting, immediate removal for non-diffusion/animation).
- `JobRegion` — Region-specific generation settings (`layer_id`, `prompt`, `bounds`, `is_background`). Important for “task splitting” scenarios where a single job may target multiple regions.
  - `JobRegion.from_dict` — Rehydrates `bounds` into a `Bounds` instance and constructs a `JobRegion`.
- `JobParams` — Main parameter bundle for a job: `bounds`, `name`, `regions`, `metadata`, `seed`, `has_mask`, `frame`, `animation_id`. This is the primary object that would carry any queue-mode or splitting-related flags if added.
  - `JobParams.from_dict` — Deserializes `bounds`/`regions` and migrates older documents (pre-1.26.0) by moving legacy top-level fields (`prompt`, `negative_prompt`, `strength`, `style`, `sampler`, `checkpoint`) into `metadata` using `_move_field`.
  - `JobParams.equal_ignore_seed` — Compares two `JobParams` ignoring `seed` (useful for detecting “same job” changes without treating reseeding as a change).
  - `JobParams.set_style` — Writes style/checkpoint/loras/sampler description into `metadata`.
  - `JobParams.prompt` (property) — Convenience accessor for `metadata["prompt"]`.
  - `JobParams.style` (property) — Convenience accessor for `metadata["style"]`.
  - `JobParams.strength` (property) — Convenience accessor for `metadata["strength"]` with default `1.0`.
- `Job` — Runtime representation of a queued/executing/finished job, including produced images and result usage tracking.
  - `Job.__init__` — Initializes job identity, kind, params, timestamp, empty `ImageCollection`, and `in_use` map.
  - `Job.result_was_used` — Checks whether a particular result index was marked used.
- `JobQueue(QObject)` — Central queue/history manager per document, exposing Qt signals for UI and other components.
  - `JobQueue.Item(NamedTuple)` — Identifies a specific result image by `(job, image)` (job id string + image index).
  - Signals:  
    - `count_changed` — queue length/state changed  
    - `selection_changed` — selection changed  
    - `job_finished` / `job_discarded` — lifecycle events for jobs  
    - `result_used` / `result_discarded` — per-result events
  - `JobQueue.__init__` — Creates `_entries` deque, selection tracking, and `_memory_usage` counter (MB).
  - `JobQueue.add` — Convenience wrapper creating a `Job(None, kind, params)` and enqueuing it.
  - `JobQueue.add_control` — Creates a `control_layer` job with a formatted name and attaches `job.control`.
  - `JobQueue.add_job` — Appends a `Job` to `_entries` and emits `count_changed`.
  - `JobQueue.remove` — Removes a job from `_entries` and emits `count_changed` (note comment: diffusion/animation are “kept for history” elsewhere; others removed on finish).
  - `JobQueue.find` — Looks up a job by `id`.
  - `JobQueue.count` — Counts jobs with an exact `state` identity match (`is state`).
  - `JobQueue.has_item` — Validates that a `JobQueue.Item` points to an existing job and result index.
  - `JobQueue.set_results` — Stores results on the job; for `JobKind.diffusion` and `JobKind.animation` it increments `_memory_usage` and calls `prune(keep=job)`.
  - `JobQueue.notify_started` — Sets state to `executing` (if not already) and emits `count_changed`.
  - `JobQueue.notify_finished` — Marks `finished`, emits `job_finished`, calls `_cancel_earlier_jobs`, emits `count_changed`, and immediately `remove`s jobs not in `[JobKind.diffusion, JobKind.animation]`.
  - `JobQueue.notify_cancelled` — Marks `cancelled`, calls `_cancel_earlier_jobs`, emits `count_changed`.
  - `JobQueue.notify_used` — Marks a specific result index as used and emits `result_used`.
  - `JobQueue.select` — Sets selection to a single `(job_id, index)` item.
  - `JobQueue.toggle_selection` — Toggles selection off/on, remembering the previous selection if still valid.
  - `JobQueue._discard_job` — Internal removal that also decrements `_memory_usage` by job results size and emits `job_discarded`.
  - `JobQueue.prune` — Discards oldest jobs while `_memory_usage > settings.history_size`, but never discards the `keep` job; uses `_entries[0]` as the oldest.
  - `JobQueue.discard` — Discards a single result image from a job (or the whole job if only one result). Shifts `in_use` flags down for subsequent indices, updates `_memory_usage`, emits `result_discarded`.
  - `JobQueue.clear` — Discards all finished `JobKind.diffusion` jobs (history clear operation); uses `_discard_job`.
  - `JobQueue.any_executing` — Returns whether any job is currently executing.
  - `JobQueue.__len__`, `JobQueue.__getitem__`, `JobQueue.__iter__` — Container-like access over `_entries`.
  - `JobQueue.selection` (property + setter) — Stores selection list and emits `selection_changed` on change.
  - `JobQueue.memory_usage` (property) — Exposes `_memory_usage` (MB).
  - `JobQueue._cancel_earlier_jobs` — Cancels any earlier jobs still `queued` or `executing` before a given job, to recover from server disconnect situations.
- `_move_field` — Helper for `JobParams.from_dict` migration: moves a key from `src` to `dest` if present.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- **Queue mode representation:** This file does **not** define an explicit “queue mode” (no bool/enum for scheduling policy). The closest concepts are `JobKind` (affects retention/removal) and `JobState` (lifecycle), plus the `_cancel_earlier_jobs` recovery behavior that implicitly changes queue semantics after disconnects.
- **Job enqueue/clear logic:** `JobQueue.add`, `JobQueue.add_job`, `JobQueue.notify_finished`, `JobQueue.remove`, and `JobQueue.clear` are the key entry points for scheduling and history management. Any change to queue mode (e.g., pause, parallelism, prioritization) would likely be implemented by augmenting these or by adding new state/fields.
- **Task splitting before client:** The presence of `JobParams.regions: list[JobRegion]` and `JobKind.animation_*` suggests splitting can be represented as multiple sub-regions or per-frame jobs, but this file itself does not implement splitting—only stores the structure and queues `Job` objects. Potential PR changes related to splitting would likely add logic around how `JobParams.regions` is populated or how multiple `Job` instances are enqueued (e.g., `animation_batch` vs `animation`).

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`ai_diffusion/api.py` defines the client/server API data structures for “workflows” (generate/inpaint/refine/upscale/control/custom), including serialization to/from JSON-compatible dicts and bundling image bytes into a single payload.

### Architecture
- The core request schema is represented by a tree of `@dataclass` models rooted at `WorkflowInput`, with supporting enums for categorical choices.
- `Serializer` converts a `WorkflowInput` into a JSON-ready `dict`, replacing `Image` objects with integer indices and packing all image bytes into a single `"image_data"` blob with offsets.
- `Deserializer` performs the inverse: reconstructs the `WorkflowInput` tree from a `dict`, rehydrating `Image` objects from the packed `"image_data"`.
- `_check_image_size()` validates image extents before serialization if a size limit is provided.
- No queue/clear/replace flags exist in this file; the closest “mode” concept is inpainting fill/mode enums and the `WorkflowKind` selection, plus `batch_count`.

### Functions & Classes
- `WorkflowKind` — `Enum` selecting the overall workflow type (`generate`, `inpaint`, `refine`, `refine_region`, `upscale_simple`, `upscale_tiled`, `control_image`, `custom`). This is the primary discriminator for request behavior and downstream logic.

- `ExtentInput` — dataclass grouping multiple extents: `input`, `initial`, `desired`, `target`. Important because these drive diffusion/upscale passes and size validation.

- `ImageInput` — dataclass holding `extent: ExtentInput` plus optional `initial_image`, `hires_image`, `hires_mask`. Central container for all images associated with a workflow.
  - `ImageInput.from_extent(e: Extent)` — convenience constructor that sets all extent fields to `e`.

- `LoraInput` — dataclass representing a LoRA attachment (`name`, `strength`, optional `storage_id` hash). Affects model conditioning payload.
  - `LoraInput.from_dict(data: dict[str, Any])` — minimal parser from dict (only reads `name` and `strength`, ignores `storage_id`).

- `CheckpointInput` — dataclass describing model selection and model-related toggles (`checkpoint`, `version: Arch`, `vae`, `loras`, `clip_skip`, `v_prediction_zsnr`, `rescale_cfg`, `self_attention_guidance`, `dynamic_caching`, `tiled_vae`). Key portion of request schema for server execution.

- `SamplingInput` — dataclass for sampler configuration (`sampler`, `scheduler`, `cfg_scale`, `total_steps`, `start_step`, `seed`).
  - `SamplingInput.actual_steps` — computed `total_steps - start_step`, used in cost estimation.
  - `SamplingInput.denoise_strength` — ratio `actual_steps / total_steps`.

- `ControlInput` — dataclass for control conditions (`mode: ControlMode`, optional `image`, `strength`, `range`). Includes an image pointer that participates in image packing.

- `RegionInput` — dataclass describing a masked region with `mask: Image`, `bounds: Bounds`, `positive` prompt, plus per-region `control` and `loras`. Used for region-based conditioning.

- `ConditioningInput` — dataclass for text/style conditioning (`positive`, `negative`, `style`, `control`, `regions`, `language`). Primary prompt/control entry point.

- `InpaintMode` — `Enum` describing semantic inpaint intent (`automatic`, `fill`, `expand`, `add_object`, `remove_object`, `replace_background`, `custom`). This “mode” is unrelated to queue/replace semantics; it’s purely image editing intent.

- `FillMode` — `Enum` describing how to fill masked areas (`none`, `neutral`, `blur`, `border`, `replace`, `inpaint`). Note that `FillMode.replace` exists here but refers to fill strategy, not request replacement in a queue.

- `InpaintParams` — dataclass holding inpaint-specific parameters (`mode`, `target_bounds`, `fill`, `grow`, `feather`, `use_inpaint_model`, `use_condition_mask`, `use_reference`).
  - `InpaintParams.clamped(self)` — returns a shallow-copied version with `grow` and `feather` clamped to `[0, 499]`.

- `UpscaleInput` — dataclass for upscale configuration (`model`, `tile_overlap`). `model == ""` signals tiled refine without an explicit upscale model.

- `CustomWorkflowInput` — dataclass enabling arbitrary workflows (`workflow: dict`, `params: dict[str, Any]`). This is the escape hatch for nonstandard request schemas.

- `WorkflowInput` — the top-level request model tying everything together:
  - Fields: `kind`, `images`, `models`, `sampling`, `conditioning`, `inpaint`, `crop_upscale_extent`, `upscale`, `control_mode`, `batch_count`, `nsfw_filter`, `custom_workflow`.
  - `WorkflowInput.extent` — convenience accessor returning `ensure(self.images).extent`.
  - `WorkflowInput.image` — convenience accessor returning `ensure(ensure(self.images).initial_image)`.
  - `WorkflowInput.upscale_factor` — computed `target.width / input.width`.
  - `WorkflowInput.from_dict(data: dict[str, Any])` — uses `Deserializer.run(data)` to parse a request dict.
  - `WorkflowInput.to_dict(self, image_format: ImageFileFormat | None = ImageFileFormat.webp, max_image_size: int = 0)` — validates via `_check_image_size()` then uses `Serializer.run(...)` to produce a payload dict (including packed image bytes if any).
  - `WorkflowInput.diffusion_extent` — chooses extent used for diffusion cost/logic: `largest(initial, crop_upscale_extent)` if crop is set, else `desired`.
  - `WorkflowInput.passes_count` — estimates number of passes: special logic for `WorkflowKind.upscale_tiled` (based on tile counts; returns `2 * max(1, tiles)`), otherwise `batch_count`.
  - `WorkflowInput.cost` — estimates a normalized cost number based on kind, architecture base cost (`_base_cost`), steps, extents, and passes. Special-cases `control_image` and `upscale_simple`.

- `_base_cost(arch: Arch)` — helper returning base cost scalar by model architecture (`sd15` => 1, `sdxl`-like => 2, `flux`-like => 4).

- `Serializer` — converts dataclass trees to dicts and packs images:
  - `Serializer.run(work: WorkflowInput, image_format: ImageFileFormat | None = ImageFileFormat.webp)` — main entry; serializes workflow; if images exist and `image_format` is not `None`, appends `"image_data": {"bytes": ..., "offsets": ...}`.
  - `Serializer.__init__(self)` — initializes internal `ImageCollection`.
  - `Serializer._object(self, obj) -> dict[str, Any]` — serializes dataclass fields, omitting `None` and values equal to their defaults.
  - `Serializer._value(self, value, default=None)` — handles `Image` (store index), `list`, default elision, `Enum` (use `.name`), `tuple` (convert to list), nested dataclasses, and primitives.

- `Deserializer` — reconstructs dataclass trees from dicts and unpacks images:
  - `Deserializer.run(data: dict[str, Any]) -> WorkflowInput` — reads optional `"image_data"` and builds `ImageCollection`, then calls `_object(WorkflowInput, data)`.
  - `Deserializer.__init__(self, images: ImageCollection)` — stores image collection for index lookups.
  - `Deserializer._object(self, type, input: dict)` — instantiates a dataclass by deserializing each field by name.
  - `Deserializer._field(self, field: Field, value)` — applies default/default_factory when `value is None`; unwraps `UnionType` by taking the first arg (notably for `T | None`), then delegates to `_value`.
  - `Deserializer._value(self, cls, value)` — handles nested dataclasses, `Enum` by name, `Image` by index, tuple conversions, `list[T]` conversions, else returns raw value.

- `_check_image_size(workflow: WorkflowInput, max_image_size: int)` — enforces a maximum longest-side limit across `input/initial/desired/target` and `workflow.crop_upscale_extent`; raises `ValueError` if exceeded.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- This file defines the **request schema** (`WorkflowInput` and nested dataclasses) and the **wire format** (`Serializer`/`Deserializer`) used between client and server. If “replace mode” changes the request schema, changes likely land here as new fields on `WorkflowInput` (or related dataclasses) and corresponding serialization behavior.
- There are **no explicit queue/clear/replace flags** in the current models. If the agent needs such flags, they would most naturally be added to `WorkflowInput` (top-level) or wrapped in a new envelope dataclass, and then ensured to round-trip via `Serializer._object` / `Deserializer._object`.
- Be aware of naming collision: `FillMode.replace` exists, but it is strictly an **inpainting fill strategy**, not a queue replacement flag—important when searching for “replace” in the codebase.

- There are **no explicit queue/clear/replace flags** in the current models. If the agent needs such flags, they would most naturally be added to `WorkflowInput` (top-level) or wrapped in a new envelope dataclass, and then ensured to round-trip via `Serializer._object` / `Deserializer._object`.
- Be aware of naming collision: `FillMode.replace` exists, but it is strictly an **inpainting fill strategy**, not a queue replacement flag—important when searching for “replace” in the codebase.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`ai_diffusion/client.py` defines the client-side abstractions and shared data structures for connecting to a backend, enqueueing workflow jobs, receiving streaming job events, and resolving model/style compatibility (including model-resource lookup and LoRA upload validation).

### Architecture
- **Event/message layer:** `ClientEvent` + `ClientMessage` define a normalized event stream interface for any concrete client implementation (local/remote), delivered via `Client.listen()`.
- **User/device/model metadata:** `User`, `DeviceInfo`, `ClientModels`, and `ModelDict` represent server/client capabilities (available models/resources, device info, user credits).
- **Client abstraction:** `Client` is an `ABC` defining the required interface (`connect`, `enqueue`, `listen`, `interrupt`, `clear_queue`, etc.) that concrete client implementations must provide.
- **Helpers for style/model selection and uploads:** `resolve_arch()`, `filter_supported_styles()`, and `loras_to_upload()` provide pre-flight logic for selecting a compatible diffusion architecture and ensuring required LoRA files exist locally and are uploadable.

### Functions & Classes
- `ClientEvent` — `Enum` of all client-emitted event types (`progress`, `finished`, `queued`, `upload`, `output`, etc.). Important because it standardizes how job lifecycle updates are communicated to UI/consumers.
- `TextOutput` — `NamedTuple` describing a textual output artifact (`key`, `name`, `text`, `mime`). Used as part of `ClientOutput`.
- `SharedWorkflow` — `NamedTuple` for a shared/published workflow (`publisher`, `workflow`). Used as part of `ClientOutput`.
- `ClientOutput` — type alias: `dict | SharedWorkflow | TextOutput`, representing heterogeneous “result payloads” accompanying some `ClientMessage` events.
- `ClientMessage` — `NamedTuple` representing a single event from `listen()`: includes `event`, optional `job_id`, `progress`, optional `images` (`ImageCollection`), optional `result` (`ClientOutput`), and optional `error`. Central to how queue/job progress is streamed.

- `User` — `QObject` + `ObservableProperties` model for user identity and counters.
  - Properties: `images_generated` and `credits` are `Property` instances with corresponding Qt signals `images_generated_changed` and `credits_changed`.
  - `__init__(self, id: str, name: str)` — sets immutable identifiers. Relevant for UI binding and credit display.

- `DeviceInfo` — `NamedTuple` summarizing compute device (`type`, `name`, `vram` GB).
  - `parse(data: dict)` — extracts device info from a backend payload (expects `data["devices"][0]`), normalizes name, computes VRAM in GB; logs error and falls back to `("cpu","unknown",0)` on failure. Important for showing hardware capability and possibly gating features.

- `MissingResources(Exception)` — exception representing missing model/custom-node requirements.
  - `__init__(self, missing: dict[Arch, list[ResourceId]] | list[CustomNode])` — stores missing requirements in either arch-keyed dict or custom-node list.
  - `__str__(self)` — fixed message: `"Required custom nodes or model files are missing"`.
  - `get(self, arch: Arch)` — returns missing list for a given `arch` (or full list if stored as list). Used by `Client.supports_arch()`.

- `CheckpointInfo` — `NamedTuple` for checkpoint metadata (`filename`, `arch`, `format`).
  - `name` (property) — returns filename without `.safetensors`.
  - `deduce_from_filename(filename: str)` — infers `Arch` via `Arch.from_checkpoint_name(filename)` and returns a `CheckpointInfo`. Helps populate `ClientModels.checkpoints`.

- `ClientModels` — container describing all models/resources accessible to the client.
  - Fields (declared): `checkpoints`, `vae`, `loras`, `upscalers`, `resources`, `node_inputs`.
  - `__init__(self)` — initializes `node_inputs` and `resources` to empty dicts (other attributes are expected to be populated externally).
  - `resource(self, kind: ResourceKind, identifier: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str, arch: Arch)` — constructs `ResourceId`, resolves via `find()`, raises if not found. Key method used by `ModelDict.__getitem__`.
  - `find(self, id: ResourceId)` — looks up `id.string` in `self.resources` with fallback rules:
    - If `id.arch is Arch.illu_v`, falls back to `Arch.illu`.
    - Then tries architecture-agnostic model (`arch=Arch.all`).
    Important for robust resource lookup across arch variants.
  - `arch_of(self, checkpoint: str)` — returns stored arch for checkpoint or deduces via `Arch.from_checkpoint_name`.
  - `for_arch(self, arch: Arch)` — returns `ModelDict(self, ResourceKind.upscaler, arch)` (a convenience entrypoint).
  - `upscale` (property) — returns `ModelDict(self, ResourceKind.upscaler, Arch.all)`.
  - `default_upscaler` (property) — resolves default upscaler resource (`UpscalerName.default`, `Arch.all`).

- `ModelDict` — helper wrapper to access `ClientModels` resources filtered by `kind` and `arch`.
  - `__init__(self, models: ClientModels, kind: ResourceKind, arch: Arch)` — stores context.
  - `__getitem__(self, key: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str)` — returns required resource path/name via `ClientModels.resource()`.
  - `find(self, key: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str, allow_universal=False)` — optional lookup with additional behavior:
    - Remaps `ControlMode.style`/`ControlMode.composition` to `ControlMode.reference` for SD (`Arch.sd15` or `arch.is_sdxl_like`).
    - Uses `ClientModels.find()` and, if `allow_universal` and the control mode allows substitution (`key.can_substitute_universal(self.arch)`), falls back to `ControlMode.universal`.
  - `for_version(self, arch: Arch)` — returns a new `ModelDict` with same kind, different arch.
  - Convenience properties returning new `ModelDict`s or resolved resources:
    - `text_encoder`, `upscale`, `control`, `ip_adapter`, `inpaint`, `lora` — return `ModelDict` for respective `ResourceKind`.
    - `clip_vision` — resolves `ResourceKind.clip_vision` `"ip_adapter"` for current arch.
    - `vae` — resolves default VAE for current arch.
    - `fooocus_inpaint` — asserts `self.arch is Arch.sdxl` and returns dict with `head` and `patch` inpaint resources.
    - `all` — returns underlying `ClientModels`.
    - `node_inputs` — returns `ClientModels.node_inputs`.
    - `has_te_vae` — checks that default VAE exists and that all required text encoders for the arch exist. Important for feature gating / validation.

- `TranslationPackage` — `NamedTuple` for translation language metadata (`code`, `name`).
  - `from_dict(data: dict)` — parses one dict.
  - `from_list(data: list)` — parses a list of dicts into `list[TranslationPackage]`.
- `ClientFeatures` — `NamedTuple` advertising server/client feature flags and limits (`ip_adapter`, `translation`, `languages`, `max_upload_size`, `max_control_layers`, `wave_speed`, `gguf`).

- `Client` — abstract base class defining the contract for concrete clients.
  - `connect(url: str, access_token: str = "") -> Client` (static, abstract, async) — establishes a connection and returns a client instance.
  - `enqueue(self, work: WorkflowInput, front: bool = False) -> str` (abstract, async) — submits a `WorkflowInput` job; `front` suggests priority insertion. Returns `job_id`.
  - `listen(self) -> AsyncGenerator[ClientMessage, Any]` (abstract) — yields `ClientMessage` updates over time.
  - `interrupt(self)` (abstract, async) — stops current job.
  - `clear_queue(self)` (abstract, async) — clears queued jobs. This is the only queue-clearing API in this file; actual “clear-queue property of first job” logic is not implemented here (it would be in a concrete `Client` subclass or in job->request conversion code elsewhere).
  - `refresh(self)` (async) — no-op default hook.
  - `translate(self, text: str, lang: str) -> str` (async) — default passthrough.
  - `disconnect(self)` (async) — no-op default.
  - `user` (property) — default `None`.
  - `missing_resources` (property) — default `None`.
  - `supports_arch(self, arch: Arch) -> bool` — returns `True` unless `missing_resources` exists and has entries for that arch.
  - `features` (property) — declared but not implemented here (must be provided by subclass).
  - `performance_settings` (property) — declared but not implemented here (must be provided by subclass).
  - `__aenter__` / `__aexit__` — async context manager; `__aexit__` calls `disconnect()`.

- `resolve_arch(style: Style, client: Client | ClientModels | None = None)` — determines which `Arch` to use for a given `Style`.
  - If `client` provided: uses `style.preferred_checkpoint(models.checkpoints.keys())` and `models.arch_of(checkpoint)` when possible.
  - Else if `style.checkpoints`: uses `style.architecture.resolve(style.checkpoints[0])`.
  - If `style.architecture.is_sdxl_like` and chosen `arch.is_sdxl_like`, it prefers `style.architecture` as a compatible user override.
  Important for ensuring downstream workflow/task building uses the correct base architecture.
- `filter_supported_styles(styles: Iterable[Style], client: Client | None = None)` — filters styles to those supported by the connected client:
  - Requires `client.supports_arch(resolve_arch(style, client))`
  - And `style.preferred_checkpoint(client.models.checkpoints.keys()) != "not-found"`
  Without `client`, returns all styles unfiltered.
- `loras_to_upload(workflow: WorkflowInput, client_models: ClientModels)` — generator yielding local LoRA files that must be uploaded because the server lacks them.
  - Collects LoRAs from `workflow.models.loras` and from each conditioning region’s `region.loras`.
  - Skips if `lora.name` already in `client_models.loras`.
  - Rejects unsupported legacy `_lcm_loras` if `lora.storage_id` is missing (raises `ValueError(_lcm_warning)`).
  - Validates `lora.storage_id` exists, local file is findable via `FileLibrary.instance().loras.find_local(lora.name)`, path exists, and hash matches (`assert lora.storage_id == lora_file.hash`).
  This is a key “job preparation” piece related to client-side submission because it determines upload requirements.

- `_lcm_loras` — list of LoRA filenames treated specially.
- `_lcm_warning` — error string instructing users to switch sampling method due to server dropping LCM support.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The PR concern (“clear-queue property of first job”) is **not implemented in this file**; what *is* here is the abstract queue API: `Client.enqueue(...)` and `Client.clear_queue()`. Any “clear queue via first job property” behavior would be implemented in a concrete subclass of `Client` (not shown here) or in code that converts `WorkflowInput` into server requests.
- This file is still directly relevant because:
  - It defines the **canonical queue-clearing mechanism** (`Client.clear_queue`) and **job submission contract** (`Client.enqueue`), which the PR might need to modify/extend.
  - `loras_to_upload()` is part of **client-side job preparation** (ensuring uploads), which often happens adjacent to request/task splitting logic.
  - `resolve_arch()` and `filter_supported_styles()` affect how workflows are parameterized prior to enqueueing, which can influence how tasks/requests are constructed downstream.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`ai_diffusion/ui/generation.py` defines the main “generation” UI panel: prompt/region controls, inpaint/refine mode selection, queue button integration, progress/error display, and a thumbnail history list with actions (apply/copy/save/discard/clear).

### Architecture
- **GenerationWidget** is the container widget composing sub-widgets (workspace/style selectors, prompt widget, strength slider, inpaint controls, generate button, queue button, progress bar, error box, and history list).
- It binds UI state to a `Model` instance via `bind`, `bind_combo`, `bind_toggle`, and Qt signals, and forwards user actions (generate/apply/save/discard/etc.) back to the model.
- **HistoryWidget** reflects `model.jobs` state (finished jobs/results) and provides selection + context-menu actions that manipulate job results and copy parameters back into the model.
- **CustomInpaintWidget** exposes inpaint-specific parameters (fill mode, seamless, focus, context selection) and binds them to `model.inpaint`.
- **ProgressBar** reflects `model.progress` and `model.progress_kind`.

### Functions & Classes

#### `HistoryWidget(QListWidget)`
A thumbnail/history browser for generated outputs, synchronized with `Model.jobs`. Critical for “apply” and “discard/clear” behaviors and for how job selection is represented in UI.

- `__init__(self, parent: QWidget | None)` — Sets up the list view (icon mode, selection rules), connects click/double-click handlers, creates overlay buttons (`_apply_button`, `_context_button`), and installs a custom context menu. Important because it defines the interactive behavior (preview click, apply, context dropdown).
- `model_(self)` (property getter) — Returns the current `Model`.
- `model_(self, model: Model)` (property setter) — Reconnects all job-related signals to the new model (`selection_changed`, `job_finished`, `job_discarded`, `result_used`, `result_discarded`), then `rebuild()`s. Important for ensuring history tracks the active model and job queue.
- `add(self, job: Job)` — Adds UI items for finished `JobKind.diffusion` (one icon per result) and `JobKind.animation` (an `AnimatedListItem`). Groups by prompt/params changes using `_last_job_params`. This is where finished queue items become visible in the UI.
- `_job_info(self, params: JobParams, tooltip_header: bool = True)` — Builds human-readable tooltip/info text from `JobParams` + metadata (style, strength, checkpoint, loras, sampler, etc.). Used for tooltips and clipboard “Info”.
- `remove(self, job: Job)` — Removes all list items for a job id (delegates to `_remove_items`).
- `remove_image(self, id: JobQueue.Item)` — Removes one specific image from a job (delegates to `_remove_items`).
- `_remove_items(self, job_id: str, image_index: int = -1)` — Core removal logic; also renumbers later image indices when removing a single result and clears selection if needed. Important for correct queue/history behavior when discarding results.
- `update_selection(self)` — Syncs UI selection to `self._model.jobs.selection` and starts/stops animation for selected `AnimatedListItem`s. Key for keeping UI selection consistent with model selection state.
- `update_apply_button(self)` — Positions/resizes/shows the floating “Apply” and context buttons near the selected item. Important for the “apply selected result” UX.
- `update_image_thumbnail(self, id: JobQueue.Item)` — Updates an item’s icon when a result is marked “used” (adds star) or otherwise changed.
- `select_item(self)` — Writes current UI selection back to `self._model.jobs.selection`. This is the main path from UI -> model for job-result selection state.
- `_toggle_selection(self)` — Calls `self._model.jobs.toggle_selection()` (bound to Space via shortcut override).
- `_activate_selection(self)` — Emits `item_activated` for the first selected item; used to apply a result on demand.
- `is_finished(self, job: Job)` — Filters which jobs should appear (only finished diffusion/animation).
- `rebuild(self)` — Clears and re-adds all finished jobs from `self._model.jobs`.
- `item_info(self, item: QListWidgetItem) -> tuple[str, int]` — Extracts `(job_id, image_index)` from item data roles. Used by apply/save/discard.
- `selected_job(self) -> Job | None` (property) — Returns the `Job` corresponding to the first selected history item, if any.
- `handle_preview_click(self, item: QListWidgetItem)` — If clicking a header item (prompt text), copies its tooltip prompt to clipboard. (Despite name, this is “copy prompt on click” behavior.)
- `mousePressEvent(self, e: QMouseEvent | None)` — Custom deselect-on-click behavior (single click on selected item clears selection without Ctrl).
- `resizeEvent(self, e)` — Updates overlay button placement on resize.
- `event(self, e: QEvent | None)` — Handles `ShortcutOverride` for Delete (discard) and Space (toggle selection) to avoid Krita conflicts. Important for keyboard-driven queue/history manipulation.
- `_find(self, id: JobQueue.Item)` — Finds the list item corresponding to a job+image id pair.
- `_item_data(self, item: QListWidgetItem)` — Packs item data into `JobQueue.Item(job_id, image_index)`.
- `_image_thumbnail(self, job: Job, index: int)` — Builds a thumbnail icon (scales, crops for minimum height, overlays star if `job.result_was_used(index)`). Important for marking “applied/used” results.
- `_show_context_menu(self, pos: QPoint)` — Builds and executes context menu: copy prompt/strength/style/seed, info-to-clipboard, save, discard, clear history. This is a primary UI surface for result management (including “clear history”).
- `_show_context_menu_dropdown(self)` — Opens the same context menu anchored under `_context_button`.
- `_copy_prompt(self)` — Applies selected job prompt to active region/root (`active.positive`, and for `RootRegion` also `active.negative`), copies prompt to clipboard, and for `Workspace.custom` tries to set custom params from metadata. Important because it shows how past jobs feed back into current generation parameters.
- `_copy_strength(self)` — Sets `self._model.strength` from selected job.
- `_copy_style(self)` — Sets `self._model.style` from selected job (if style exists).
- `_copy_seed(self)` — Forces fixed seed and sets `self._model.seed` from selected job.
- `_info_to_clipboard(self)` — Copies `_job_info(..., tooltip_header=False)` to clipboard.
- `_save_image(self)` — Calls `self._model.save_result(job_id, image_index)` for each selected item. Important for persistence behavior tied to job results.
- `_discard_image(self)` — Calls `self._model.jobs.discard(job_id, image_index)` for each selected item; tries to keep a sensible current row afterward. Important for queue/history mutation from UI (Delete shortcut and menu).
- `_clear_all(self)` — Confirmation dialog, then `self._model.jobs.clear()` and `self.clear()`. This is the UI entry point for clearing the entire job history/queue results.

#### `AnimatedListItem(QListWidgetItem)`
Represents an animation result as a single list item that cycles through icons.
- `__init__(self, images: list[QIcon])` — Stores frames, sets `UserType`, configures a `QTimer`.
- `start_animation(self)` — Starts the timer (40ms) to cycle frames.
- `stop_animation(self)` — Stops timer, resets to first frame.
- `_next_frame(self)` — Advances frame and updates icon.

#### `CustomInpaintWidget(QWidget)`
UI for custom inpaint parameters; binds directly to `model.inpaint` and reacts to architecture/strength/style.
- `__init__(self, parent: QWidget)` — Creates checkboxes and combos for seamless/focus/fill/context; wires `context_combo.currentIndexChanged` to `set_context()`.
- `model(self)` (property getter) — Returns current model.
- `model(self, model: Model)` (property setter) — Rebinds UI to model: `bind_combo(model.inpaint, "fill", ...)`, `bind_toggle(... "use_inpaint")`, `bind_toggle(... "use_prompt_focus")`, and listens to model changes to refresh enabled state and context layers.
- `update_widgets_enabled(self)` — Enables/disables controls based on `arch` and `model.strength`. Important gating: `fill_mode_combo` only when `strength == 1.0`; seamless depends on SDXL/controlnet-inpaint; focus depends on SD15/SDXL-like.
- `update_context_layers(self)` — Rebuilds context combo entries for mask layers (`self._model.layers.masks`), preserving current selection if possible.
- `update_context(self)` — Reflects `model.inpaint.context`/`context_layer_id` into the combo selection.
- `set_context(self)` — Writes combo selection back to `model.inpaint.context` (layer bounds vs enum context).

#### `ProgressBar(QProgressBar)`
Thin progress indicator tied to model progress.
- `__init__(self, parent: QWidget)` — Configures range/appearance.
- `model(self)` (property getter) — Returns current model.
- `model(self, model: Model)` (property setter) — Connects to `progress_changed` and `progress_kind_changed`.
- `_update_progress_kind(self)` — Switches highlight color for `ProgressKind.upload`.
- `_update_progress(self)` — Sets value from `model.progress` (0..1 mapped to 0..1000) or animates an indeterminate-ish progress when `progress < 0`.

#### `GenerationWidget(QWidget)`
Main panel assembling all generation controls and wiring them to the model. Most relevant entry point for “where queue mode is chosen” and “click handlers” that trigger generation.
- `__init__(self)` — Builds the full UI: workspace/style selectors, `RegionPromptWidget`, `StrengthWidget`, region/control buttons, `CustomInpaintWidget`, `GenerateButton`, inpaint-mode dropdown menus, region-only mask toggle, `QueueButton`, `ProgressBar`, `ErrorBox`, and `HistoryWidget`. Connects `history.item_activated` to `apply_result()` and calls `update_generate_button()`.
- `model(self)` (property getter) — Returns current model.
- `model(self, model: Model)` (property setter) — Central binding/wiring:
  - Binds `workspace`, `style`, `strength`, `error`, and `region_only` to UI widgets.
  - Hooks many model/document/region signals to `update_generate_button()`.
  - Hooks button clicks: `add_control_button.clicked.connect(model.regions.add_control)`, `add_region_button.clicked.connect(model.regions.create_region_group)`.
  - Hooks generation triggers: `self.region_prompt.activated.connect(model.generate)` and `self.generate_button.clicked.connect(model.generate)`.
  - Assigns sub-widget models: `custom_inpaint.model`, `generate_button.model`, `queue_button.model`, `progress_bar.model`, `strength_slider.model`, `history.model_`.
  This is the key place where “Generate” submits work (via `model.generate`) and where queue-related UI (`QueueButton`) is attached to the model.
- `apply_result(self, item: QListWidgetItem)` — Extracts `(job_id, index)` and calls `self.model.apply_generated_result(job_id, index)`. This is the UI entry point for applying a queued/generated result back into the document.
- `_mk_action(self, mode: InpaintMode, text: str, icon: str)` — Creates an `QAction` that triggers `change_inpaint_mode(mode)`.
- `_create_inpaint_menu(self)` — Menu containing all `InpaintMode` options.
- `_create_generate_region_menu(self)` — Menu for “Generate Region” variants (automatic/custom).
- `_create_refine_menu(self)` — Menu for “Refine” variants (automatic/custom).
- `_create_refine_region_menu(self)` — Menu for “Refine Region” variants.
- `_create_edit_menu(self)` — Menu for edit-arch flows (automatic/custom).
- `show_inpaint_menu(self)` — Chooses which menu to show based on `model.arch.is_edit`, `model.strength`, and `model.region_only`, and displays it under the generate button. This is where the UI decides between generate/refine/edit modes.
- `change_inpaint_mode(self, mode: InpaintMode)` — Sets `self.model.inpaint.mode`.
- `toggle_region_only(self, checked: bool)` — Sets `self.model.region_only` (note: not directly connected here; `bind_toggle` handles the UI->model link).
- `update_generate_button(self)` — Computes visibility/icon/text for generate button and inpaint/custom widgets based on:
  - whether there’s a selection (`model.document.selection_bounds`)
  - whether “region only” is active and valid (regions exist and active layer linked)
  - `model.arch.is_edit`
  - `model.strength`
  - resolved mode (`model.resolve_inpaint_mode()`)
  Sets `self.generate_button.operation` and icon accordingly, and toggles visibility of `inpaint_mode_button` and `custom_inpaint`. Important for understanding which operation a click will submit and what parameters are exposed before job submission.

#### `_region_mask_button_icons`
Maps boolean “region-only active” to icons.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- **Job submission triggers / click handlers:** `GenerationWidget.model` wires both `self.generate_button.clicked` and `self.region_prompt.activated` to `model.generate`, which is the key UI path that submits work (and therefore interacts with queue behavior downstream).
- **Queue behavior exposure:** This file doesn’t implement queue append/clear modes itself, but it *does* host and attach `QueueButton` (`self.queue_button.model = model`), making `GenerationWidget` the UI integration point where queue mode selection likely appears (inside `QueueButton` implementation).
- **Clear/discard behaviors:** `HistoryWidget._discard_image()` calls `self._model.jobs.discard(...)` and `HistoryWidget._clear_all()` calls `self._model.jobs.clear()`. These are the main UI entry points that mutate job history/results and are directly relevant to “append/clear” expectations.
- **Properties passed into job creation:** Not constructed here directly; instead, UI writes parameters into `Model` (style/strength/inpaint settings/region_only/prompt), then calls `model.generate`. For PR work affecting job creation inputs, the relevant bindings are in `GenerationWidget.model` and parameter-copy helpers in `HistoryWidget` (e.g., `_copy_prompt()`, `_copy_strength()`, `_copy_style()`, `_copy_seed()`).

### File Overview
`ai_diffusion/ui/generation.py` defines the main “generation” UI panel: prompt/region controls, inpaint/refine mode selection, queue button integration, progress/error display, and a thumbnail history list with actions (apply/copy/save/discard/clear).

### Architecture
- **GenerationWidget** is the container widget composing sub-widgets (workspace/style selectors, prompt widget, strength slider, inpaint controls, generate button, queue button, progress bar, error box, and history list).
- It binds UI state to a `Model` instance via `bind`, `bind_combo`, `bind_toggle`, and Qt signals, and forwards user actions (generate/apply/save/discard/etc.) back to the model.
- **HistoryWidget** reflects `model.jobs` state (finished jobs/results) and provides selection + context-menu actions that manipulate job results and copy parameters back into the model.
- **CustomInpaintWidget** exposes inpaint-specific parameters (fill mode, seamless, focus, context selection) and binds them to `model.inpaint`.
- **ProgressBar** reflects `model.progress` and `model.progress_kind`.

### Functions & Classes

#### `HistoryWidget(QListWidget)`
A thumbnail/history browser for generated outputs, synchronized with `Model.jobs`. Critical for “apply” and “discard/clear” behaviors and for how job selection is represented in UI.

- `__init__(self, parent: QWidget | None)` — Sets up the list view (icon mode, selection rules), connects click/double-click handlers, creates overlay buttons (`_apply_button`, `_context_button`), and installs a custom context menu. Important because it defines the interactive behavior (preview click, apply, context dropdown).
- `model_(self)` (property getter) — Returns the current `Model`.
- `model_(self, model: Model)` (property setter) — Reconnects all job-related signals to the new model (`selection_changed`, `job_finished`, `job_discarded`, `result_used`, `result_discarded`), then `rebuild()`s. Important for ensuring history tracks the active model and job queue.
- `add(self, job: Job)` — Adds UI items for finished `JobKind.diffusion` (one icon per result) and `JobKind.animation` (an `AnimatedListItem`). Groups by prompt/params changes using `_last_job_params`. This is where finished queue items become visible in the UI.
- `_job_info(self, params: JobParams, tooltip_header: bool = True)` — Builds human-readable tooltip/info text from `JobParams` + metadata (style, strength, checkpoint, loras, sampler, etc.). Used for tooltips and clipboard “Info”.
- `remove(self, job: Job)` — Removes all list items for a job id (delegates to `_remove_items`).
- `remove_image(self, id: JobQueue.Item)` — Removes one specific image from a job (delegates to `_remove_items`).

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
Implements a custom PyQt5 toggle switch UI control (`SwitchWidget`) derived from `QAbstractButton`, with animated thumb movement and custom painting.

### Architecture
- `SwitchWidget` is a checkable `QAbstractButton` that draws its own “track” and “thumb” in `paintEvent()`.
- The thumb position is represented by a Qt property (`offset`) so it can be animated via `QPropertyAnimation` when the user toggles the switch.
- The widget computes sizing/radii from font metrics and updates thumb position on resize and on programmatic checked-state changes.

### Functions & Classes
- `class SwitchWidget(QAbstractButton)` — Custom toggle/switch button widget; core UI component providing checkable state, drawing, and animation.
  - `__init__(self, parent=None)` — Configures the button as checkable, fixed-size policy, computes geometry constants (`_thumb_radius`, `_track_radius`, `_margin`, `_base_offset`), defines `_end_offset` lambdas for checked/unchecked thumb positions, initializes colors from the palette, and sets `_track_opacity`.
  - `offset(self)` (decorated with `@pyqtProperty(int)`) — Getter for the animated thumb position property; required so `QPropertyAnimation` can animate it.
  - `offset(self, value)` (via `@offset.setter`) — Setter that updates `_offset` and triggers a repaint (`update()`); crucial for smooth animation frames.
  - `sizeHint(self)` — Returns a fixed preferred size based on track/thumb radii and margins; influences layout sizing.
  - `is_checked(self)` (property) — Convenience wrapper around `isChecked()` for Pythonic access.
  - `is_checked(self, checked)` (setter) — Sets checked state via `super().setChecked(checked)` and immediately snaps `offset` to the corresponding end position; ensures correct thumb placement when toggled programmatically.
  - `resizeEvent(self, a0)` — Calls base implementation and recomputes `offset` for current checked state; keeps thumb aligned after resizes.
  - `paintEvent(self, e)` — Performs all custom drawing: track as a rounded rect and thumb as an ellipse; chooses colors/opacity based on enabled state and checked state.
  - `mouseReleaseEvent(self, e)` — On left mouse release, creates a `QPropertyAnimation` targeting `b"offset"` from current `offset` to `_end_offset[self.isChecked()]()` over 120ms, then starts it; provides the toggle slide animation.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- This file defines a generic toggle switch control and does **not** contain any queue-specific logic, “QueueButton” implementation, alternate queue modes, or modifier handling (e.g., Ctrl-click). The only click handling is in `mouseReleaseEvent(self, e)`, which checks for `Qt.MouseButton.LeftButton` but does not inspect keyboard modifiers (`e.modifiers()`), so ctrl-click alternate queue mode is not implemented here. If `QueueButton` is implemented “as a switch,” it likely composes or subclasses `SwitchWidget` elsewhere, where modifier-based behavior would need to be added.

## Source Code:
"""Toggle switch widget
from https://stackoverflow.com/a/51825815
"""

from PyQt5.QtCore import QPropertyAnimation, QSize, Qt, pyqtProperty  # type: ignore
from PyQt5.QtGui import QPainter
from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QAbstractButton, QSizePolicy


class SwitchWidget(QAbstractButton):
    def __init__(self, parent=None):
        super().__init__(parent=parent)
        self.setCheckable(True)
        self.setSizePolicy(QSizePolicy.Fixed, QSizePolicy.Fixed)

        self._thumb_radius = self.fontMetrics().height() // 2
        self._track_radius = self._thumb_radius + 2

        self._margin = max(0, self._thumb_radius - self._track_radius)
        self._base_offset = max(self._thumb_radius, self._track_radius)
        self._end_offset = {
            True: lambda: self.width() - self._base_offset,
            False: lambda: self._base_offset,
        }
        self._offset = self._base_offset

        palette = self.palette()
        self._thumb_color = {
            True: palette.text(),
            False: palette.light(),
        }
        self._track_color = {
            True: palette.highlight(),
            False: palette.dark(),
        }
        self._text_color = {
            True: palette.highlight().color(),
            False: palette.dark().color(),
        }
        self._track_opacity = 1

    @pyqtProperty(int)
    def offset(self):  # type: ignore
        return self._offset

    @offset.setter  # type: ignore
    def offset(self, value):
        self._offset = value
        self.update()

    def sizeHint(self):
        return QSize(
            4 * self._track_radius + 2 * self._margin,
            2 * self._track_radius + 2 * self._margin,
        )

    @property
    def is_checked(self):
        return self.isChecked()

    @is_checked.setter
    def is_checked(self, checked):
        super().setChecked(checked)
        self.offset = self._end_offset[checked]()

    def resizeEvent(self, a0):
        super().resizeEvent(a0)
        self.offset = self._end_offset[self.isChecked()]()

    def paintEvent(self, e):
        p = QPainter(self)
        p.setRenderHint(QPainter.Antialiasing, True)
        p.setPen(Qt.PenStyle.NoPen)
        track_opacity = self._track_opacity
        thumb_opacity = 1.0
        if self.isEnabled():
            track_brush = self._track_color[self.isChecked()]
            thumb_brush = self._thumb_color[self.isChecked()]
        else:
            track_opacity *= 0.8
            track_brush = self.palette().shadow()
            thumb_brush = self.palette().mid()

        p.setBrush(track_brush)
        p.setOpacity(track_opacity)
        p.drawRoundedRect(
            self._margin,
            self._margin,
            self.width() - 2 * self._margin,
            self.height() - 2 * self._margin,
            self._track_radius,
            self._track_radius,
        )
        p.setBrush(thumb_brush)
        p.setOpacity(thumb_opacity)
        p.drawEllipse(
            self.offset - self._thumb_radius,
            self._base_offset - self._thumb_radius,
            2 * self._thumb_radius,
            2 * self._thumb_radius,
        )

    def mouseReleaseEvent(self, e):
        super().mouseReleaseEvent(e)
        if e and e.button() == Qt.MouseButton.LeftButton:
            anim = QPropertyAnimation(self, b"offset", self)
            anim.setDuration(120)
            anim.setStartValue(self.offset)
            anim.setEndValue(self._end_offset[self.isChecked()]())
            anim.start()
summarization.summary:
### File Overview
Implements a custom PyQt5 toggle switch UI control (`SwitchWidget`) derived from `QAbstractButton`, with animated thumb movement and custom painting.

### Architecture
- `SwitchWidget` is a checkable `QAbstractButton` that draws its own “track” and “thumb” in `paintEvent()`.
- The thumb position is represented by a Qt property (`offset`) so it can be animated via `QPropertyAnimation` when the user toggles the switch.
- The widget computes sizing/radii from font metrics and updates thumb position on resize and on programmatic checked-state changes.

### Functions & Classes
- `class SwitchWidget(QAbstractButton)` — Custom toggle/switch button widget; core UI component providing checkable state, drawing, and animation.
  - `__init__(self, parent=None)` — Configures the button as checkable, fixed-size policy, computes geometry constants (`_thumb_radius`, `_track_radius`, `_margin`, `_base_offset`), defines `_end_offset` lambdas for checked/unchecked thumb positions, initializes colors from the palette, and sets `_track_opacity`.
  - `offset(self)` (decorated with `@pyqtProperty(int)`) — Getter for the animated thumb position property; required so `QPropertyAnimation` can animate it.
  - `offset(self, value)` (via `@offset.setter`) — Setter that updates `_offset` and triggers a repaint (`update()`); crucial for smooth animation frames.
  - `sizeHint(self)` — Returns a fixed preferred size based on track/thumb radii and margins; influences layout sizing.
  - `is_checked(self)` (property) — Convenience wrapper around `isChecked()` for Pythonic access.
  - `is_checked(self, checked)` (setter) — Sets checked state via `super().setChecked(checked)` and immediately snaps `offset` to the corresponding end position; ensures correct thumb placement when toggled programmatically.
  - `resizeEvent(self, a0)` — Calls base implementation and recomputes `offset` for current checked state; keeps thumb aligned after resizes.
  - `paintEvent(self, e)` — Performs all custom drawing: track as a rounded rect and thumb as an ellipse; chooses colors/opacity based on enabled state and checked state.
  - `mouseReleaseEvent(self, e)` — On left mouse release, creates a `QPropertyAnimation` targeting `b"offset"` from current `offset` to `_end_offset[self.isChecked()]()` over 120ms, then starts it; provides the toggle slide animation.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- This file defines a generic toggle switch control and does **not** contain any queue-specific logic, “QueueButton” implementation, alternate queue modes, or modifier handling (e.g., Ctrl-click). The only click handling is in `mouseReleaseEvent(self, e)`, which checks for `Qt.MouseButton.LeftButton` but does not inspect keyboard modifiers (`e.modifiers()`), so ctrl-click alternate queue mode is not implemented here. If `QueueButton` is implemented “as a switch,” it likely composes or subclasses `SwitchWidget` elsewhere, where modifier-based behavior would need to be added.

class QueueButton(QToolButton):
    def __init__(self, supports_batch=True, parent: QWidget | None = None):
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._model = root.active_model
        self._connect_model()

        self._popup = QueuePopup(supports_batch)
        popup_action = QWidgetAction(self)
        popup_action.setDefaultWidget(self._popup)
        self.addAction(popup_action)

        self.setToolButtonStyle(Qt.ToolButtonStyle.ToolButtonTextBesideIcon)
        self.setPopupMode(QToolButton.ToolButtonPopupMode.InstantPopup)
        self._update()

    @property
    def model(self):
        return self._model

    @model.setter
    def model(self, model: Model):
        if self._model != model:
            Binding.disconnect_all(self._connections)
            self._model = model
            self._popup.model = model
            self._connect_model()

    def _connect_model(self):
        self._connections = [
            self._model.jobs.count_changed.connect(self._update),
            self._model.progress_kind_changed.connect(self._update),
        ]

    def _update(self):
        count = self._model.jobs.count(JobState.queued)
        queued_msg = _("{count} jobs queued.", count=count)
        cancel_msg = _("Click to cancel.")

        if self._model.progress_kind is ProgressKind.upload:
            self.setIcon(theme.icon("queue-upload"))
            self.setToolTip(_("Uploading models.") + f" {queued_msg} {cancel_msg}")
            count += 1
        elif self._model.jobs.any_executing():
            self.setIcon(theme.icon("queue-active"))
            if count > 0:
                self.setToolTip(_("Generating image.") + f" {queued_msg} {cancel_msg}")
            else:
                self.setToolTip(_("Generating image.") + f" {cancel_msg}")
            count += 1
        else:
            self.setIcon(theme.icon("queue-inactive"))
            self.setToolTip(_("Idle."))
        self.setText(f"{count} ")

    def sizeHint(self) -> QSize:
        original = super().sizeHint()
        width = original.height() * 0.75 + self.fontMetrics().width(" 99 ") + 20
        return QSize(int(width), original.height())

    def paintEvent(self, a0):
        _paint_tool_drop_down(self, self.text())
